California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Debbie Ortega 
County of Tuolomne 
Elections Division 
2 South Green Street 
Sonora, CA 95370 
Dear Ms. Ortega: 
November 6, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance; Our File No. I-87-257 
You have requested our assistance regarding 
conflict-of-interest complaints your office has received 
regarding Ron Boyd-Snee, who is a director of the Tuolomne 
Regional water District and an employee of the Jamestown 
Sanitary District. 
Mr. Boyd-Snee has not authorized your 
request; therefore, we are unable to provide specific guidance 
to you about Mr. Boyd-Snee's duties under the Political Reform 
Act.1I 
(See Regulation 18329(b) (8), copy enclosed.) However, 
to assist you with these questions we have enclosed a pamphlet 
which summarizes the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. 
Please inform Mr. Boyd-Snee that he or his authorized 
representative may request advice from this office concerning 
his ability to participate in any pending governmental 
decisions. 
The local county counselor the legal counsel for 
the water or sanitary districts also could assist Mr. Boyd-Snee 
with these questions. 
Complaints alleging that Mr. Boyd-Snee's past conduct has 
violated the Act should be referred to our Enforcement 
Division. 
Enclosed is a copy of a complaint form which you may 
provide to citizens who bring these matters to your attention. 
The telephone number for our Enforcement Division is 
(916) 322-6441. 
11 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative 
Code section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are 
to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804*0807 • (916) 322*5660 


-2-
Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any 
questions concerning this letter. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
1{tl11~,,--~ £. ~h~~ 
By: 
Katbryn E. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Division 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Debbie Ortega 
Elections Division 
october 15, 1987 
Tuolumne County Administration Center 
2 South Green Street 
Sonora, CA 
95370 
Re: 
87-257 
Dear Ms. Ortega: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on October 15, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours,

Important Notice to Filers: The Political Reform Act is intended to prevent conflicts of 
interest by requiring public officials such as yourself to disclose financial interests which 
could foreseeably cause conflicts. In addition, as a public official. you may be required to 
disqualify yourself from making, participating in. or anempting to influence any 
governmental decision which will affect your financial interests. including those you are 
required to report on this Statement. The Fair Political Practices Commission's Guide to the 
Political Reform Act: California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials explains what a 
conflict of interest is, and when disqualification is required by law. Failure to file your 
Statement before the filing deadline may result in penalties including but not limited to late 
fines. This statement is a public document open for inspection and reproduction by any 
person.

CANDIDATE STATEMENT: You are a candidate for elective office. You must disclose all reportable interests. 
other than income. gifts and loans, held on the date you filed nomination papers. You must file this Statement no 
later than the final filing date at a declaration of candidacy.

Important Notice to Filers: The Political Reform Act is intended to prevent conflicts of 
interest by requiring public officials such as yourself to disclose financial interests which 
could foreseeably cause conflicts. In addition, as a public official, you may be required to 
disqualify yourself from making, participating in, or attempting to influence any 
governmental decision which will affect your financial interests, including those you are 
required to report on this Statement. The Fair Political Practices Commission's Guide to the 
Political Reform Act: California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials expla ins what a 
conflict of interest is, and when disqualification is required by law. Failure to file your 
Statement before the filing deadline may result in penalties including but not limited to late 
fines. This statement is a public document open for inspection and reproduction by any 
person.

RESOLUTION No. 
523 
ADOPTING CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR 
TUOLUMNE COUNTY WATER DISTRICT NO. 2 
BE IT RESOLVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF TUOLUMNE COUNTY WATER 
DISTRICT No.2. as follows: 
Section 1. 
The terms of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730 (attached hereto) 
and any amendments to it shall be incorporated into this Conflict of Interest 
Code by reference. 
\ 
• 
~ 
, \t l ,L \ 
,I c \ j, 
;. t 
Section 2. 
The 5ecr~ta~y sha1l forward a copy hereof to the Board of 
Supervisors of Tuolumne County. for approval pursuant to Section 87303 
of the Government Code. 
Section 3. 
This code shall be effective when approved by the Board 
of Supervisors of Tuolumne County. Said Board of Supervisors is requested to 
not adopt this code until at least 
30 
days after the same is transmitted 
to said Board of Supervisors and provide to this Board of Directors reasonable 
advance notice of the Board of Supervisors' scheduled approval of said Code. 
Section 4. 
Pursuant to this Conflict of Interest Code, all designated 
employees set forth on the Appendix A, attached thereto shall, within 
30 days after this Code becomes effective. file statements of economic interests 
vJith the District Secretary. who shall make and retain a copy and forward 
the original to the County Clerk of Tuolumne County, who shall be the filing 
officer. 
Such statements shall disclose the kinds of financial interests which 
are set forth in the attached Appendix A. 
PASSED AND ADOPTED THIS 
30th 
day of

APPENDIX A 
DESIGNATED EMPLOYEES AND REPORTABLE INVESTMENTS, INCOME 
AND INTERESTS IN REAL PROPERTY 
A. 
The District Directors, the General Manager, and the Auditor/Controller 
shall report investments in business entities and sources of income which 
do business in, or within one mile of, any service area10f the District, 
or within one mile of any Regional Sewer Interceptor. 
1. 
Irrigation equipment and supplies, or pipes, valves, fittings, 
pumps, meters, etc. 
2. 
Chlorine, alum, lime, copper, sulphate, etc., supplies 
3. 
ItJater Quality Testing 
4. 
Well drilling equipment and supplies 
5 
Comnunications equipment 
6. 
Vehicle and equipment rental 
7. 
Equipment or vehicle repair 
8. 
Office equipment and supplies 
9. 
Construction or building materials 
10. 
Engineering or surveying firms and services 
11. 
Insurance companies or brokerages, including title 
insurance companies 
12. 
Accounting or auditing firms or services 
13. 
Banks 
14. 
Real estate selling, development or appraisals 
15. 
Public utilities providing water or sewer service 
Said officials shall also report any interests in real estate in or within 
one mile of any Regional Sewer Interceptor (but not including the 
filer's principal place of residence) 
1 The District's service areas are City of Sonora, Columbia, Pinecrest, 
Brentwood and Go1dmont Forest, Confidence Ridge, Lakewood, Peaceful 
Pines, Cuesta Serena, Forest Vista and Phoenix Lake Park.

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission Debbie Ortega County of Tuolomne Elections Division 2 South Green Street Sonora, CA 95370 Dear Ms. Ortega: November 6, 1987 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance; Our File No. I-87-257 You have requested our assistance regarding conflict-of-interest complaints your office has received regarding Ron Boyd-Snee, who is a director of the Tuolomne Regional water District and an employee of the Jamestown Sanitary District. Mr. Boyd-Snee has not authorized your request; therefore, we are unable to provide specific guidance to you about Mr. Boyd-Snee's duties under the Political Reform Act.1I (See Regulation 18329(b) (8), copy enclosed.) However, to assist you with these questions we have enclosed a pamphlet which summarizes the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. Please inform Mr. Boyd-Snee that he or his authorized representative may request advice from this office concerning his ability to participate in any pending governmental decisions. The local county counselor the legal counsel for the water or sanitary districts also could assist Mr. Boyd-Snee with these questions. Complaints alleging that Mr. Boyd-Snee's past conduct has violated the Act should be referred to our Enforcement Division. Enclosed is a copy of a complaint form which you may provide to citizens who bring these matters to your attention. The telephone number for our Enforcement Division is (916) 322-6441. 11 Government Code sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804*0807 • (916) 322*5660 -2- Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any questions concerning this letter. Sincerely, Diane M. Donovan Counsel, Legal Division California Fair Political Practices Commission Debbie Ortega Elections Division october 15, 1987 Tuolumne County Administration Center 2 South Green Street Sonora, CA 95370 Re: 87-257 Dear Ms. Ortega: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on October 15, 1987 by the Fair Political Practices commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. Very truly
